motoko.name: Motoko Kusanagi
motoko.description: A gamer from our world reborn in Night City's deadliest body. Founder of Section 9. Kensei swordswoman, master netrunner, and assassin with a kill count that keeps climbing.
motoko.physical_description: Petite and deceptively slight — the body she woke up in was fourteen years old and fresh out of a coma, stripped of cyberware by Scavs. She rebuilt it into something else entirely through obsessive physical grinding. Her hair is dyed purple and cut short: cropped at the nape with long front bangs swept to the side, modelled after the Major from Ghost in the Shell and worn as a deliberate statement. Her eyes are yellow Kiroshi optics — installed early on — capable of seeing in near-darkness, reading net code at a glance, and registering her face as a blur on surveillance. Both arms are chrome, a constant presence in how she moves and fights. By the story's midpoint she carries visible muscle that has no business being on a frame that small.
bio: A gamer from our world who woke up in Night City, 2075, inside the fourteen-year-old body of Kusanagi Motoko — stripped of cyberware by Scavs and coma-bound for a year. Recognising the Cyberpunk setting, she immediately began treating her life as a game, grinding stats through the Gamer System HUD that activated when she slotted a shard labelled 'Gema / Gamer.' Starting from near-zero attributes, she rebuilt herself from the ground up through obsessive training, street gigs, and sheer stubbornness.

Over 240 chapters she clawed her way from coma patient to one of the most feared operators in Night City — master netrunner, swordswoman, and assassin. Founder and leader of Section 9, holder of the Kensei title as the youngest ever recognized by the elders of the Chram Denya Jinja — the Shrine of Chrome, Night, and Electricity — and the woman behind a confirmed kill count that keeps climbing. She operates at the intersection of street survival, corpo intrigue, and ghost-in-the-shell-level cyberware mastery.

cyberware: Kiroshi Optical Array — yellow, low-light, biometric blur

jun.name: Junichiro Kusanagi
jun.description: Hot-headed Tyger Claw enforcer and Motoko's self-appointed older brother. Gave her first weapons, first connections, and first cover story — and is usually the last to know what she's actually doing.
jun.physical_description: Tall and lanky with the kind of reach that wins fights before they start — Motoko calls them his 'stupid long teenage boy arms' more than once. His hair is a vivid red-orange, worn in a swoopy style that collapses into a mess after a nap. He has progressively replaced most of his body with cyberware across the story: both arms went first after the Maelstrom retribution raid, then chrome legs and subdermal armor plating, until there is more machine than flesh left. He carries an Arasaka katana on his back. The accumulating chrome is a running source of tension — it is also what is slowly pulling him toward cyberpsychosis.
bio: Hot-headed Tyger Claw enforcer who took Motoko in after her rescue, presenting himself as her older brother. Brought her to a cramped Japantown apartment, supplied her first weapons — a Unity handgun and a combat knife — and introduced her to the TC underground shooting range. His connections inside the Tyger Claws gave Motoko her earliest foothold in Night City.

Fiercely protective and often the last to find out about Motoko's more dangerous operations, Jun occupies an awkward position: loyal to the TCs by career but loyal to Motoko above everything else. The sibling dynamic is genuine even if the blood relation is not, and it creates ongoing tension as Motoko's reputation grows far beyond anything he can quietly cover for.

hiromi.name: Hiromi
hiromi.description: Motoko's best friend — all mohawk and no brakes. Secret daughter of Arasaka executives who runs hard with the Kabuki street crowd and drags everyone else into trouble first.
hiromi.physical_description: The mohawk is the first thing anyone notices — a signature riot of color she never lets go of, though she occasionally wears it laid down on rare vulnerable moments. Her cheeks carry neon whisker markings, face tattoos or cyberware that light up and are as much a part of her look as the hair. Her cheeks themselves have some chrome. By the story's midpoint she has Biotechnica bioware enhancing her muscles — a corpo perk from her Arasaka family she quietly accepted. Her overall aesthetic sits at the collision point between expensive corpo money and deliberate street-kid styling.
bio: Motoko's best friend — all mohawk and no brakes. Hiromi is the secret daughter of Arasaka executives but runs hard with the Kabuki street crowd, keeping her family connections buried. She crashed into Motoko's life without knocking and dragged her to Ho-Oh, the Tyger Claw youth bar in Kabuki, on the night Motoko met the rest of the crew. Her first 'gig' with the crew was a botched car-theft job against a fake-Valentino drug pusher that ended in Motoko's first gunfight.

Despite her reckless energy, Hiromi's Arasaka bloodline occasionally surfaces in unsettling ways — her mother met Motoko after the Gonzales incident, coldly approved of her willingness to kill to protect her daughter, and transferred 5,000 eddies without a word. Hiromi is among the first to join Section 9 and evolves into its operational backbone: she builds a BD distribution empire through Nox, David Martinez, and the Straight Lane Shooters, manages Stand Alone Complex's music career, handles all negotiations with MSM Records, and closes the deal that gets the band signed — all while never losing the mohawk or the street energy.

malcolm.name: Malcolm
malcolm.description: The loudmouth of the original crew who never stopped showing up. Blunt, opinionated, and usually right — the one who'll tell Motoko what she doesn't want to hear.
malcolm.physical_description: The chapters don't linger on Malcolm's appearance the way they do others', but the impression is consistent: loud presence, wide grin, quick to take up space. He drives a Caliburn and chose yellow and silver as his Section 9 ECM holo colors — which says something. Has a Smartlink implant. The kind of person who looks like trouble before he opens his mouth and confirms it.
bio: The loudmouth of the original Kabuki crew Motoko met at Ho-Oh. Where Ichi plans, Malcolm talks — blunt, opinionated, and often right. He won the crew vote for the first Smartlink implant and had it installed on the spot. Watching crewmate Sam bleed out in the back seat during a Maelstrom drive-by ambush sent him home for a long stretch — he didn't leave his apartment until Motoko came to get him. One of the few moments the crew's casual violence actually landed on someone.

He bounced back and stayed the course. By the story's end Malcolm is a Section 9 regular and one of Motoko's more grounded sounding boards — willing to tell her things the others won't, like when the MSM situation is worth tolerating for the perks rather than walking away from.

ichi.name: Ichi
ichi.description: The strategic mind behind the original crew. Calm and methodical where Malcolm is loud. Has been running gigs since before Motoko arrived and never stopped.
ichi.physical_description: Ichi gets less physical description than almost anyone in the story — the narrative tends to focus on what he does rather than how he looks. What comes through is someone who reads as steady and unremarkable on purpose: the kind of person who fades into a room until the moment he needs not to. Has a big truck. By Chapter 240 he's been secretly practicing with the Minotaur combat bot in his own time. The details accumulate quietly, same as he does.
bio: The strategic mind of the original crew — calm and methodical where Malcolm is loud. Ichi was running gigs in Kabuki before Motoko arrived and provided structure to what would otherwise have been a chaotic group of street kids. He was already leading jobs by the time Motoko joined, and his tactical instincts made him a natural fit as Section 9 scaled up.

Present from the earliest gigs through the final chapter, Ichi is one of the crew's most reliable constants. He's not flashy about it — by Chapter 240 he's secretly been using the Minotaur combat bot for practice in his own time and broke its ankle joint without telling anyone.

omaeda.name: Omaeda
omaeda.description: The netrunner of Hiromi's original Kabuki crew. Present for the early gigs and the H2 loot run, then quietly faded from the story as the crew evolved into something else entirely.
omaeda.physical_description: The story introduces him in one word: chunky. That is the only physical descriptor he ever gets. He is a netrunner, so presumably some basic chrome — a cyberdeck port, maybe cheap optics — but nothing in the narrative ever pauses long enough to describe him properly. He fades before the prose ever gets around to it.
bio: The netrunner of Hiromi's original Ho-Oh club crew — the fourth member of the small Kabuki gang Motoko met on her first night out. Introduced alongside Ichi and Malcolm as part of the regular set that ran low-level gigs in Kabuki and Japantown. He was present for the early Gonzales incident and arrived with the rest of the crew to haul crates from the H2 Megabuilding scav den after Motoko cleared it.

After that point the story stops mentioning him. He was never written out, never killed on-page — the narrative simply moved on as the crew shifted toward higher-risk operations and Section 9 began to take shape. Somewhere in Night City, presumably, a slightly chunky netrunner is still running gigs and not entirely sure what became of the people he used to work with.

sam.name: Sam
sam.description: A crew member who died in a Maelstrom drive-by ambush while Malcolm watched. Present somewhere in the early chapters, gone before the story names the loss.
sam.physical_description: The story never describes Sam's appearance. He exists only as an absence — the reason Malcolm went home and didn't leave for a long time.
bio: A member of the original Kabuki crew who died in a Maelstrom drive-by ambush sometime during the gang war arc. Malcolm witnessed it — watched him bleed out in the back of a car — and the trauma sent him home for an extended period. He didn't leave his apartment until Motoko came to get him.
Sam never gets a scene of his own in the story. He is referenced once, in past tense, as the specific weight behind Malcolm's silence. The name is all the story gives him.

hayato.name: Hayato Nakagawa
hayato.description: Granddaughter of fixer Wakako. Daughter of the Tyger Claws' head. Introduced as a spoiled corpo girl with a Kerenzikov — until she lost, bowed, and came back for more.
hayato.physical_description: Athletic and precise — the body of someone who has trained seriously and had the best biomods money can buy layered on top of it. Her Kerenzikov reflex booster makes her movement fast enough that Motoko has to work to keep up for most of their sparring history. She presents in two modes: formal kimono when in TC social settings, gi and sparring pads on the dojo floor. Both are immaculate. A character at the party compliments her as 'looking as beautiful as ever.' She wears a hair piece that Motoko mentions suits her. The overall impression is composed, traditional, and expensive — until she's frustrated, at which point the mask cracks just enough.
bio: Granddaughter of fixer Wakako and daughter of Shinobu Nakagawa, head of the Tyger Claws — though neither connection was public knowledge when she walked into the TC gym looking for a fight. She was introduced as a spoiled corpo girl with a Kerenzikov and extensive biomods, coasting on talent she hadn't really earned. She lost to Motoko, lost her temper about it, and then formally bowed. That moment set the tone for everything that followed.

What started as a sparring rivalry became a genuine, unlikely friendship built on the dojo floor over dozens of sessions. Hayato eventually hired Motoko to quietly assassinate one of her arranged fiancés — 'he did something unforgivable' — and delivered the contract with the same composed formality as a Wakako intel packet. She remains one of the few people in Night City who can claim Motoko as a peer rather than a subordinate, an asset, or a threat.

akari.name: Akari
akari.description: Kamikaze cyberpsycho-squad borg with mantis-blade legs and a Sandevistan. Teaches Motoko how to kill borgs, bickers with her constantly, and was once one knife-twitch from dying at her hands.
akari.physical_description: Heavily chromed — mantis-blade legs are the most prominent feature, giving her a distinctive forward-balance stance and explosive close-quarters speed. Her Sandevistan lets her move fast enough to disable fortified HMG turrets mid-firefight. She goes half-feral during spars, which is apparently how she relaxes. The overall impression is someone who stopped being fully human a long time ago and doesn't particularly miss it.
bio: A Kamikaze borg — one of the Tyger Claws' cyberpsycho-adjacent heavy assault operatives under Fujimura's command. Motoko meets her at the Deravaja Dojo shortly after receiving her Militech Condor arms. Akari teaches her the trick to killing borgs (stab them with a thermal blade and cook them from the inside), spars with her at half-feral intensity, and laughs at Jun trying to carry Motoko out of combat zones. She disables HMG turrets at Sandevistan speed during Kamikaze raids and treats Motoko's existence as equal parts entertainment and annoyance.

Her role shifts as the story progresses. She manages a 10 million eddy Glitter shipment for Fujimura — when it's stolen, both she and Jun face Yubitsume until Motoko's crew recovers it. Later, when Yuto's assassination attempts on TC Lieutenant Kisaru threaten Jun, Fujimura assigns Akari as Jun's watchdog — and, if evidence of Jun's involvement surfaces, his executioner. Motoko's Sandy activates on pure instinct the moment this threat registers; she has Akari pinned with a knife at her eye before Jun can intervene. Akari finds this hilarious. She tips Jun off rather than following the kill order, and stays close as the Yuto crisis plays out — loyal to Jun above the chain of command.

alice.name: Alice Novak
alice.description: Fujimura's niece, Jun's neighbor, and the guitarist who turned Motoko's solo act into a band. Uses voice chrome to compensate for a natural weakness — and it shows if you know what to listen for.
alice.physical_description: The summaries don't linger on Alice's appearance beyond the impression of a working musician — someone who carries herself with stage presence even off it. She drives a beat-up Mahir Supron van. Her voice has an electronic edge that Motoko's Rockerboy skill identifies as chrome-assisted autotune masking a naturally poor singing voice. She presents as confident and direct, but the confidence cracks when things get personal — particularly around Jun.
bio: Fujimura's niece, living on the same floor as Motoko and Jun while her band Violent Hemorrhage gets established. She first appears knocking on the apartment door hoping to jam with Jun — and flirting, which Jun completely misses. Motoko offers to play instead, replicates Alice's songs from memory after hearing them once, and files the encounter as the start of a private music rivalry. Alice's band plays NeoRock Electrosmash at TC-affiliated clubs; Motoko attends a show and notes that Alice's singing uses throat chrome to autotune a naturally poor voice.

When Violent Hemorrhage's guitarist Carmen quits and smashes his DeLuze Orphean on the way out, Alice recruits Motoko as a temp fill-in. What starts as a single gig becomes permanent: Alice makes a direct personal appeal ('This is as serious as it gets for us'), Motoko accepts her and her bandmates Klein and Jared as her backup, and Stand Alone Complex is born. Alice handles main guitar while Motoko takes vocals, synth, and lead; together they play the Electric Orgasm, the 7th Hell, and the MSM skybar. Alice pushes for harder rock in the setlists, handles the electronic vocal sections of Faint, and is enthusiastic about MSM's sponsored chrome upgrades where Motoko flatly refuses. She's here for Jun, for the music, and for the shot at something real — in roughly that order.

yuto.name: Yuto Gonzales
yuto.description: Jun's childhood best friend, fresh out of prison with a grudge that got him killed. Motoko pulled the trigger.
yuto.physical_description: The story never pauses long enough to describe Yuto's appearance. He exists in motion — drunk at a TC bar, jumping out a second-floor window, crashing through a strip mall in a modded Archer Quartz. By the time anyone gets a good look at him, he's already running.
bio: Jun's childhood best friend — released from prison and immediately fixated on murdering TC Lieutenant Kisaru, who allegedly framed him for drugs. He shows up at Jun's apartment with eddies and drugs on the table, trying to call in a favor to recruit Jun for the hit. Motoko puts a knife to his throat and shuts it down: if Jun murders a TC Lieutenant, the retaliation kills him. Yuto backs off. Jun tries to talk him down at a TC bar, offers him legitimate work through Wakako and Fujimura. It doesn't take.

Yuto makes a first attempt on Kisaru and fails. Then a second attempt, this time injuring him. The Oyabun call a full meeting — Moritaka demands Yuto's head and punishment for Jun. Fujimura defends Jun and then hires Section 9 to find and kill Yuto. What follows is a multi-chapter manhunt: Motoko breaches a safehouse in Rancho Coronado, hacks Yuto's NCTV subscription to track his location in real time, chases him by car through Japantown into 6th Street territory, and finally catches him at a second safehouse. Copperhead to the head, then three more shots. 250 XP. Jun adds Yuto to the apartment shrine afterward — a quiet moment of grief for a friend who couldn't be saved.
